description: Processes the child nodes with a higher samplerate

parameterGroups.label: Oversampling Configuration
parameterGroups.rationale: Both parameters configure the oversampling engine: the factor selects how much to oversample, and the filter type selects the anti-aliasing filter used during up/downsampling.

properties.note: UI-only: controls whether the container is displayed vertically or horizontally in the scriptnode graph editor. No effect on signal processing.

gaps.question: The oversample container wraps DynamicSerialProcessor. Does it process children serially (like chain) within the oversampled context? Confirm the wrapper composition: wrap::oversample<-1, DynamicSerialProcessor>.
gaps.context: Needed to describe the signal flow: input -> upsample -> serial child chain at higher rate -> downsample -> output.

gaps.question: The Oversampling parameter has range 0-4 with step 1. From infrastructure we know this is an exponent (0=1x, 1=2x, 2=4x, 3=8x, 4=16x). The TextToValueConverter is present. Confirm the exact mapping and whether value 0 (1x) means no oversampling (passthrough) or still processes through the JUCE oversampler with factor 1.
gaps.context: Users need to know if setting Oversampling=0 (1x) adds any latency or CPU overhead vs not using the container at all.

gaps.question: FilterType has range 0-1. From infrastructure: 0=PolyphaseIIR (lower latency), 1=FIR Equiripple (steeper rolloff). Confirm the exact display names from the TextToValueConverter and verify there are exactly 2 options.
gaps.context: Reference page needs accurate filter type names and practical guidance on when to choose each.

gaps.question: Does the oversample container report latency to the host? JUCE dsp::Oversampling has getLatencyInSamples(). Is this propagated to the DspNetwork's latency reporting?
gaps.context: Users need to know if oversampling introduces latency and whether it is automatically compensated.

gaps.question: From containers.md: OversampleNode overrides setBypassed() to re-prepare with original specs when bypass changes. Confirm: when bypassed, do children revert to the original sampleRate and blockSize?
gaps.context: Important for documentation: bypassing the oversample container changes the processing context for all children.

gaps.question: When the Oversampling parameter changes at runtime, the wrapper uses SimpleReadWriteLock and calls prepare() again. Does this cause an audible gap (silence during re-preparation) or is it seamless? Is the JUCE oversampler rebuilt or just reconfigured?
gaps.context: Users need to know if changing the oversampling factor at runtime is safe during playback or should only be done during initialization.

gaps.question: The oversample wrapper in process() uses NUM_MAX_CHANNELS (16). Is there a practical channel limit for the oversample container?
gaps.context: Documentation should note any channel count restrictions.

notes: This is the BASE VARIANT of the oversample group. It is the dynamic version (OversampleFactor template parameter = -1 / registered as 0 in the factory), exposing both Oversampling and FilterType parameters. The fixed variants (oversample2x-16x) have the factor baked at compile time and only expose FilterType. The isPolyphonic=false classification comes from wrap::oversample which explicitly throws Error::IllegalPolyphony -- this is a hard constraint, not just a default. processFrame() asserts false, so this container only supports block-based processing.
